meaning: 鑾遍】[鑽峰叞瑗块儴鍩庡競]
examples: Fewer than half of the group's members elected to leave Leiden.
He went to the Leiden Medical School, because he wanted to see how you dissected bodies.
Aya Ezawa is a sociologist in the Japanese studies program at Leiden University in the Netherlands.
Prince Willem-Alexander was educated in Wales and Holland, where he earned a history degree at Leiden University.
In the Old World, a decision by a group of Leiden Separatists put them on a circuitous journey.
